title: Re-summarise a completed MNL run and emit user-facing CSV exports
summary: Recompute the FitSummary from a completed run and write the user-facing artifacts: summary.json with coefficients, log-likelihood, AIC/BIC; plus a CSV exports directory containing per-prefix CSV exports (coefficient table, optional utilities). Idempotent — safe to re-run.
description: Reads the persisted state from a completed MNL `fit` run and writes the user-facing artifacts: a `summary.json` with coefficients, log-likelihood, and information criteria, plus a CSV exports directory containing the coefficient table with standard errors. Does not refit anything — purely a post-processing step. Safe to re-run any number of times.
usage:
[["model-lg::finalize"]]
run_dir = "<existing run dir>"
run_name = "lg_run"
# optional:
export_dir = "<dir>"

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Directory of a completed MNL fit run. Must contain `spec.json` and `fit.postcard` — the persisted state written by `fit`.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: export_dir
arguments.description: Where to write the CSV exports. Defaults to `{run_dir}/exports`. Override only if you want to keep multiple finalisations side-by-side.

details:
`finalize` is the post-processing step that turns a raw MNL fit checkpoint into a stakeholder-ready summary. It reads `fit.postcard` and computes:

- The coefficient table (point estimates plus standard errors derived from the inverse Hessian).
- Log-likelihood at the fitted point.
- Information criteria (AIC, BIC).
- Convergence diagnostics from the final optimizer state.

The persisted state itself is left untouched. `fit` calls `finalize` automatically at the end of a successful run with the default `export_dir`, so you don't normally need to invoke this command explicitly. Use it directly when you want to write exports to a different directory.

The `coefficients.csv` output is the file you pass to `choice-wrangling::expand-coefficients` to translate the fitted coefficients back into attribute / level form for reporting.
value:
On success, `export_dir` (defaulting to `{run_dir}/exports`) contains:

- `summary.json` — coefficients, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC, convergence diagnostics.
- `coefficients.csv` — the coefficient table with standard errors, ready for `choice-wrangling::expand-coefficients`.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: `finalize` does not refit — it only re-summarises the persisted state.
Use `export_dir` to keep multiple finalisations side-by-side without overwriting each other.
The `coefficients.csv` output is the file you pass to `choice-wrangling::expand-coefficients` for human-readable reporting.

see_also.description: Initial MNL fit — calls `finalize` automatically at the end.
Cross-validation counterpart — finalises every fold of a `cv-fit` run.
Translate fitted coefficients back into attribute / level form for reporting.

examples.title: Write exports to a side-by-side directory
Re-finalise a CV fold run to inspect its coefficient table

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Directory of a completed fit run (must contain spec.json + fit.postcard).

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: export_dir
fields.description: Where to write the CSV exports. Defaults to {run_dir}/exports.

artifacts.title: Coefficient table
artifacts.description: Re-exported aggregate MNL coefficients with standard errors. Ready for choice-wrangling::expand-coefficients.

artifacts.title: Fit summary
artifacts.description: Coefficients, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC, and convergence status in JSON form.
